pi_items.source: HTTP request to URL returned HTTP 200 with valid HTML content-type
Path is consistent across setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and task.json metadata
String matches task description and is valid HTML meta tag syntax
task_utils.sh line 3 and 261 confirm 'This environment uses REAL websites'

pi_items.metadata_value: Verifier assumes: 'If agent searches HTML scope, they will find results on crawler-test.com'
pi_items.verified_value: CONTRADICTED: No <meta name="keywords"> tags found on any pages sampled from crawler-test.com
pi_items.source: Downloaded homepage (1103 lines HTML), sampled 77 random pages from 391 total internal links, explicitly tested 4 specific pages. All searches returned 0 matches for '<meta name="keywords"' in any scope.

privileged_info_summary: The task targets crawler-test.com, a real, public SEO testing website (HTTP 200 OK). The target URL, domain, export path, and search term are all correctly specified and consistent across all task scripts. However, investigation reveals a critical contradiction: the verifier logic assumes that searching for '<meta name="keywords"' in HTML source on crawler-test.com will yield hits (Count > 0). In reality, no <meta name="keywords"> tags were found on any of the tested pages from crawler-test.com. This means the success criteria cannot be met even if an agent correctly configures Screaming Frog to search in 'HTML' scope, making the task impossible to pass as currently designed.
